text: Hello. This is 6 Minute English from BBC Learning English.
zh: [0] 你好,欢迎收听BBC英语教学的《六分钟英语》。

text: I'm Neil. And I'm Beth. In this episode we're talking about twins.
zh: [1] 我是尼尔。我是贝丝。本期节目我们要聊聊双胞胎。

text: Non -identical or fraternal twins come from two separate eggs and share half their genes.
zh: [2] 异卵双胞胎来自两个不同的卵子,只共享一半的基因。

text: More uncommon are identical twins who come come from a single fertilised egg and share 100 % of their genes.
zh: [3] 较为少见的是同卵双胞胎,他们来自同一个受精卵,基因100%相同。

text: As humans, we're fascinated by twins.
zh: [4] 作为人类,我们对双胞胎总是充满好奇。

text: When people discover that someone's a twin, they start asking all kinds of questions.
zh: [5] 当人们发现某人是双胞胎时,总会开始问各种各样的问题。

text: What question would you ask a twin, Neil? Oh, probably quite an annoying one. Maybe, do you share each other's thoughts?
zh: [6] 尼尔,你会问双胞胎什么问题?哦,可能是个挺讨人厌的问题。也许是,你们会分享彼此的想法吗?

text: Ooh, I might ask, if one of you is hurt, does the other one feel the pain?
zh: [7] 哦,我可能会问,如果你们中一个受伤了,另一个会感觉到疼吗?

text: Interesting. Well, some common questions for twins include,
zh: [8] 有意思。其实,人们常问双胞胎的问题包括,

text: do you have psychic powers? Have you ever swapped boyfriends? And do you have the same dreams?
zh: [9] 你们有心灵感应能力吗?你们交换过男朋友吗?你们会做一样的梦吗?

text: But according to Anand Jagatia, who presented a recent episode
zh: [10] 不过据阿南德·贾加蒂亚说——他主持了最近一期

text: of BBC World Service's Crowd Science all about twins...
zh: [11] BBC世界频道《群体科学》节目,主题就是双胞胎……

text: If you meet a twin and you ask them one of these questions, don't be surprised if they roll their eyes at you.
zh: [12] 如果你遇到一对双胞胎,问了这些问题中的一个,别惊讶他们会对你翻白眼。

text: Ask a twin about psychic powers and they might roll their eyes – a way of showing boredom or irritation.
zh: [13] 问双胞胎关于心灵感应的问题,他们可能会翻白眼——这是一种表示厌烦或恼怒的方式。

text: But nevertheless, twins continue to capture our imagination
zh: [14] 但尽管如此,双胞胎依然持续激发着我们的想象力,

text: and in this episode we'll be finding out why, using some useful new words and phrases.
zh: [15] 在本期节目中,我们将通过一些实用的新词汇和短语,一起探究其中的原因。

text: And remember, you'll find all the vocabulary for this episode,
zh: [16] 记住,你可以在我们的网站上找到本期所有词汇,

text: along with a quiz and a worksheet on our website bbclearningenglish .com. First I have a question for you, Beth.
zh: [17] 还有配套的小测验和练习题,网址是bbclearningenglish.com。首先,我有个问题要问贝丝。

text: These days, twins are more common than you might think. In fact, one in every 42 babies is born a twin.
zh: [18] 如今,双胞胎比你想象的要常见得多。事实上,每42个婴儿中就有一个是双胞胎。

text: But do you know which famous Hollywood star is a twin?
zh: [19] 但你知道哪位著名的好莱坞明星是双胞胎吗?

text: Is it a Jennifer Lawrence, b Angelina Jolie, or c Scarlett Johansson?
zh: [20] 是a詹妮弗·劳伦斯,b安吉丽娜·朱莉,还是c斯嘉丽·约翰逊?

text: Ooh, I don't know. I'm going to guess Scarlett Johansson.
zh: [21] 哦,我不知道。我猜是斯嘉丽·约翰逊。

text: Well, we'll find out the answer at the end of the programme.
zh: [22] 好,我们节目最后会揭晓答案。

text: Professor Karen Dillon is the author of a book on twins in American literature and a twin herself.
zh: [23] 凯伦·狄龙教授是一本关于美国文学中双胞胎的书的作者,她本人也是双胞胎之一。

text: Here she discusses the good and bad sides of twinship
zh: [24] 在这里,她与阿南德·贾加蒂亚一起,

text: with Anand Jagatia for BBC World Service programme, CrowdScience. Being a twin isn't always plain sailing.
zh: [25] 为BBC世界频道节目《群体科学》讨论了做双胞胎的利与弊。做双胞胎并不总是一帆风顺。

text: Yeah, it is nice. There's never that fear because you're going through school together, you're sometimes in the same class together,
zh: [26] 是的,这确实很好。你不会有那种恐惧感,因为你们一起上学,有时还在同一个班,

text: you're making friends together. So all of those milestones of childhood in some ways are made easier for twins.
zh: [27] 一起交朋友。所以童年的这些重要阶段,对双胞胎来说在某种程度上会更容易度过。

text: But then the older The older you get, the more you realise how much people really think of you as a twin rather than as an individual.
zh: [28] 但随着年龄增长,你会越来越意识到,人们其实更多地把你当作双胞胎中的一个,而不是一个独立的个体。

text: Twins go through many childhood milestones together.
zh: [29] 双胞胎会一起经历许多童年的重要阶段。

text: A milestone is an important stage of development, so a childhood milestone could be something like your first day at school.
zh: [30] milestone(里程碑)指的是发展过程中的重要阶段,所以童年的一个里程碑可能就是你第一天上学。

text: But being a twin isn't always plain sailing – an idiom meaning easy and problem -free.
zh: [31] 但做双胞胎并不总是一帆风顺——plain sailing这个习语的意思是轻松、没有问题。

text: Many twins find it hard to establish their own identity as a separate and unique individual.
zh: [32] 许多双胞胎发现,要建立自己独立、独特的身份认同很难。

text: Our fascination with twins also comes from the age -old nature versus nurture debate.
zh: [33] 我们对双胞胎的着迷,也源于那个古老的“天性与教养”之争。

text: How much of our lives is determined by nature, the genetics we're born with,
zh: [34] 我们的人生有多少是由天性——也就是我们与生俱来的基因——决定的?

text: and how much by nurture, the environment we grow up in?
zh: [35] 又有多少是由教养——也就是我们成长的环境——决定的?

text: Because twins are so genetically similar, differences between them tell scientists a lot.
zh: [36] 因为双胞胎在基因上如此相似,他们之间的差异能给科学家提供大量信息。

text: His presenter of BBC World Service programme CrowdScience, Anand Jagatia,
zh: [37] BBC世界频道节目《群体科学》的主持人阿南德·贾加蒂亚,

text: discussing this with Helena de Bresse, a philosopher at Wellesley College, Massachusetts.
zh: [38] 与马萨诸塞州韦尔斯利学院的哲学家海伦娜·德布雷斯就此展开了讨论。

text: And that's right, another twin. Yeah, you hear about twins that were separated at birth, grew up in different parts of the world,
zh: [39] 没错,她也是一对双胞胎中的一个。是的,你会听说有些双胞胎一出生就被分开,在世界不同的地方长大,

text: and they kind of have these eerily similar life courses.
zh: [40] 结果他们的人生轨迹却诡异地相似。

text: And you think, gosh, like how much choice have I had over the way that my life's turned out?
zh: [41] 你会想,天哪,我的人生走向,自己到底能有多少选择的余地?

text: Absolutely, yeah. There's that really famous case of the Jim twins who were reunited at age 39
zh: [42] 确实如此。有一个很著名的案例,就是“吉姆双胞胎”,他们39岁时才重逢,

text: after being separated at birth and they just had so many similarities. It was crazy.
zh: [43] 出生后就被分开,可他们之间却有那么多相似之处,简直不可思议。

text: They both married someone called Linda and then divorced her for someone called Betty.
zh: [44] 他们都娶了一个叫琳达的女人,后来又都为了一个叫贝蒂的女人和她离婚。

text: They both named their dog Toy. It was like ridiculous.
zh: [45] 他们都给自己的狗取名叫Toy。简直太荒唐了。

text: The twins Jim Lewis and Jim Springer were separated at birth.
zh: [46] 双胞胎吉姆·刘易斯和吉姆·斯普林格出生后就被分开了。

text: The phrase separated at birth can be used to describe two people with strong similarities,
zh: [47] separated at birth(出生时分离)这个说法可以用来形容两个极其相似的人,

text: although in this case the twins were literally separated and adopted by different parents at birth.
zh: [48] 不过在这个案例中,这对双胞胎是真的在出生时就被分开,并被不同的家庭收养。

text: When the Jims were reunited in the 1970s, everyone was amazed at how similar their lives were.
zh: [49] 20世纪70年代,两个吉姆重逢时,所有人都惊讶于他们人生的相似程度。

text: Anand used the adjective eerie, meaning strange in a frightening, mysterious way.
zh: [50] 阿南德用了形容词eerie,意思是那种令人恐惧、神秘莫测的怪异感。

text: And Helena says it was crazy, using crazy to mean strange and fantastic.
zh: [51] 海伦娜说这“太疯狂了”,crazy在这里的意思是奇怪又不可思议。

text: Yes, I think strange, mysterious and fantastic describe being a twin quite well.
zh: [52] 是啊,我觉得“奇怪”“神秘”和“不可思议”这几个词,挺能形容做双胞胎的感受。

text: OK, Neil, it's time to reveal the answer to your question.
zh: [53] 好了,尼尔,是时候揭晓你那个问题的答案了。

text: I asked which Hollywood star is a twin and you said C. Scarlett Johansson.
zh: [54] 我问的是哪位好莱坞明星是双胞胎,你说是c,斯嘉丽·约翰逊。

text: I did. Which was the right answer. Yay! Let's recap the vocabulary we've learned, starting with
zh: [55] 我是这么说的。而这正是正确答案。太棒了!让我们来回顾一下今天学到的词汇,首先是——

text: roll your eyes, to move your eyes upwards to show you are bored or annoyed.
zh: [56] roll your eyes,翻白眼,指向上转动眼睛,以表示厌烦或恼怒。

text: A milestone is a significant stage in the development of something.
zh: [57] milestone(里程碑)指某事物发展过程中的重要阶段。

text: The idiom, it's not plain sailing, describes difficult and problematic situations.
zh: [58] 习语it's not plain sailing,形容困难重重、问题不断的情况。

text: The phrase separated at birth can describe two people who are unusually similar.
zh: [59] separated at birth这个说法可以形容两个异常相似的人。

text: It also refers to siblings who were adopted by different parents at birth.
zh: [60] 它也指那些出生时就被不同家庭收养的兄弟姐妹。

text: And the adjective eerie means strange in a frightening and mysterious way.
zh: [61] 形容词eerie意思是令人恐惧、神秘莫测的怪异。

text: And finally, if someone says it was crazy, they mean it was strange and fantastic.
zh: [62] 最后,如果有人说it was crazy,他们的意思是这件事既奇怪又不可思议。

text: Once again, our six minutes are up. But remember, you can find worksheets, quizzes and loads more resources
zh: [63] 我们的六分钟又到时间了。不过别忘了,你可以在我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com上

text: to improve your English on our website, bbclearningenglish .com. See you there soon, but for now, Goodbye.
zh: [64] 找到练习题、小测验以及更多提升英语水平的资源。我们很快在那里见,现在,再见。
